title: Sail membranes for optomechanical accelerometry
abstract: Strained membrane resonators have emerged as a promising platform for optomechanical accelerometry; however, the desired combination of low frequency and high $Q$-mass product requires a rethinking of their dissipation dilution engineering. Applying Bayesian optimization to a Si$_3$N$_4$ membrane, we discover a class of sail-like trampoline resonators in which the frequency is decreased by an order of magnitude while preserving the $Q$-mass product. We demonstrate centimeter-scale sails with kHz frequencies, $Q\sim10^7$ and $Q\times\text{mass}\sim$ 10 g. Vertically integrating a 7 kHz device with a nanoribbon, we realize a monolithic cavity optomechanical accelerometer with a room temperature thermal noise of $40\;\text{n}g_0/\sqrt{\text{Hz}}$, sufficient to resolve $μg_0/\sqrt{\text{Hz}}$ ambient vibration over a bandwidth of 4 kHz with a displacement imprecision of $10^{-14}\;\text{m}/\sqrt{\text{Hz}}$. Cryogenic arrays of sail membranes may be attractive for new physics searches and distributed quantum sensing experiments.

bite.hook: A new sail-like trampoline resonator achieves a room temperature thermal noise of just 40 n_g0/rt-Hz.
tldr: They designed a new class of sail-like trampoline resonators to optimize optomechanical accelerometers.
They applied Bayesian optimization to a silicon nitride membrane and vertically integrated a 7 kHz device with a nanoribbon.
They demonstrated centimeter-scale sails with Q-factors of ~10^7, a Q-mass product of ~10 g, and a displacement imprecision of 10^-14 m/rt-Hz.
bite.whyItMatters: This ultra-sensitive accelerometer could enable extremely precise navigation without GPS, improve distributed quantum sensing, and help search for new physics like dark matter.
bite.eli5: Imagine a tiny trampoline that is so sensitive it can feel the vibration of a single dust mote landing nearby. By shaping this trampoline like a ship's sail, scientists made it incredibly light and flexible while keeping it from losing its energy, allowing it to measure tiny changes in speed.

glossary.shortDef: The study of the interaction between light and mechanical systems.
glossary.eli5Def: Using light beams to push, pull, or measure the movement of tiny physical objects.

glossary.shortDef: A sequential design strategy for global optimization of black-box functions.
glossary.eli5Def: A smart trial-and-error guessing game where a computer uses past results to make better guesses about the best design.

glossary.shortDef: A dimensionless parameter that describes how underdamped an oscillator is.
glossary.eli5Def: A number that tells you how long something keeps vibrating; a high number means it rings for a long time like a high-quality bell.
